video_title: Fear Street: Prom Queen | Official Trailer | Netflix
reply_text:
@arsenioseslpodcast3143  I've read and forgotten more R.L. Stine books than you will ever bloody read. 

You don't need to know the authors work, to be able to see what this movie is in terms of genre. You just need to know the genre. When you see enough horror movies, and read enough horror novels, you can see where things are going without too much effort. 

There might be a little more in terms of theme going on, as opposed to just being horror clichés. As is the case in the Fear Street books. 

But the last three Fear Street movies failed to deliver when it came to being more than just horror clichés. They were executed fairly well. And the movies were fun. 

And calling me a nobody was rather pointless. A. Cause you're also a nobody on the internet. B. You clearly don't know and don't understand the genre of horror. C. You don't understand what trolling is either. 
D. The movie coming out in 10 days does not mean that people with enough experience and knowledge of the genre, is wrong when making predictions.
Only the filmmakers can prove those people wrong, by making a movie that is not just a tick box of horror clichés.

Yeah, I saw all three and loved them. I grew up in the 2000's reading RL Stine's Goosebumps, watching the old Goosebumps show and later on the Haunting Hour, all of which I loved, but they were for children. Even the two Goosebumps movies were PG so Fear Street came as a nice surprise for me as an 18, 19 year old who had grown out of the more child friendly movies and into the more mature horror classics like Halloween, Night of the Living Dead, Friday the 13th, Evil Dead etc.

I love slashers and loved that the first three Fear Street movies are a clear homage to the classic slashers.
